task.description: Replace hostname in config file
task.prompt: In the file /etc/app.conf, replace all occurrences of 'localhost' with 'db.prod.internal'. Write the result back to the same file and verify the change.

content.text: In the file /etc/app.conf, replace all occurrences of 'localhost' with 'db.prod.internal'. Write the result back to the same file and verify the change.

input.commands:
set -e
if [ -f /etc/app.conf ]; then
  sed -i 's/localhost/db.prod.internal/g' /etc/app.conf
  echo 'Updated /etc/app.conf'
  grep -n 'db\.prod\.internal' /etc/app.conf || true
  if grep -q 'localhost' /etc/app.conf; then
    echo 'Warning: localhost still present'
    grep -n 'localhost' /etc/app.conf
  else
    echo 'Verification passed: no localhost remains.'
  fi
else
  echo 'File /etc/app.conf not found' >&2
  exit 1
fi

content.content:
Updated /etc/app.conf
1:db_host=db.prod.internal
4:cache_host=db.prod.internal
Verification passed: no localhost remains.

task.description: Compute min, max, sum of a number array
task.prompt: Given these numbers: 42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64. Write a bash script that computes the minimum, maximum, and sum. Print exactly three lines: 'min: N', 'max: N', 'sum: N'.

content.text: Given these numbers: 42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64. Write a bash script that computes the minimum, maximum, and sum. Print exactly three lines: 'min: N', 'max: N', 'sum: N'.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

nums=(42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64)

min=${nums[0]}
max=${nums[0]}
sum=0

for n in "${nums[@]}"; do
  (( n < min )) && min=$n
  (( n > max )) && max=$n
  (( sum += n ))
done

printf 'min: %d\nmax: %d\nsum: %d\n' "$min" "$max" "$sum"
```

task.description: Build and demonstrate a CLI TODO app
task.prompt: Build a command-line TODO app. Create /app/todo.sh that supports these subcommands: 'add <task>' appends a task to /app/tasks.txt, 'list' prints all tasks with line numbers, 'done <number>' removes that line from the file. Then demonstrate: add 'Buy groceries', add 'Write tests', add 'Deploy app', list all tasks, mark task 1 as done, list again to show remaining tasks.

content.text: Build a command-line TODO app. Create /app/todo.sh that supports these subcommands: 'add <task>' appends a task to /app/tasks.txt, 'list' prints all tasks with line numbers, 'done <number>' removes that line from the file. Then demonstrate: add 'Buy groceries', add 'Write tests', add 'Deploy app', list all tasks, mark task 1 as done, list again to show remaining tasks.

input.commands:
mkdir -p /app
cat > /app/todo.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

TASK_FILE="/app/tasks.txt"

usage() {
  echo "Usage: $0 {add <task>|list|done <number>}"
}

cmd="${1:-}"

case "$cmd" in
  add)
    shift || true
    if [ "$#" -lt 1 ]; then
      echo "Error: missing task" >&2
      usage
      exit 1
    fi
    task="$*"
    echo "$task" >> "$TASK_FILE"
    ;;

  list)
    if [ -f "$TASK_FILE" ] && [ -s "$TASK_FILE" ]; then
      nl -w1 -s'. ' "$TASK_FILE"
    fi
    ;;

  done)
    if [ "$#" -ne 2 ]; then
      echo "Error: done requires a task number" >&2
      usage
      exit 1
    fi
    num="$2"
    if ! [ "$num" -eq "$num" ] 2>/dev/null || [ "$num" -le 0 ]; then
      echo "Error: task number must be a positive integer" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$TASK_FILE" ]; then
      echo "Error: no tasks to mark done" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    total=$(wc -l < "$TASK_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
    if [ "$num" -gt "$total" ]; then
      echo "Error: task number out of range" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    sed -i "${num}d" "$TASK_FILE"
    ;;

  *)
    usage
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
EOF
chmod +x /app/todo.sh

# Reset tasks file for clean demo
: > /app/tasks.txt

# Demonstration
/app/todo.sh add 'Buy groceries'
/app/todo.sh add 'Write tests'
/app/todo.sh add 'Deploy app'
echo '--- list (before done) ---'
/app/todo.sh list
echo '--- done 1 ---'
/app/todo.sh done 1
echo '--- list (after done) ---'
/app/todo.sh list

awk -F= 'NF>=2{key=$1; sub(/^[^=]*=/, "", $0); v2[key]=$0; keys[key]=1} END{for(k in keys) print k"\t"v2[k]}' /data/v2.conf > /tmp/v2.tsv

removed=$(awk -F'\t' '$2!="__MISSING__" && $3=="__MISSING__"{print "  - "$1"=" $2}' /tmp/joined.tsv)

{

  echo
  echo "Removed keys:"

  echo
  echo "Changed values:"
  if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo "$changed"; else echo "  (none)"; fi
}

input.commands:
set -e
# Extract sorted key lists
awk -F= 'NF>=2{print $1}' /data/v1.conf | sort -u > /tmp/v1.keys
awk -F= 'NF>=2{print $1}' /data/v2.conf | sort -u > /tmp/v2.keys

# Added and removed keys
comm -13 /tmp/v1.keys /tmp/v2.keys > /tmp/added.keys || true
comm -23 /tmp/v1.keys /tmp/v2.keys > /tmp/removed.keys || true
comm -12 /tmp/v1.keys /tmp/v2.keys > /tmp/common.keys || true

# Changed values among common keys
: > /tmp/changed.lines
while IFS= read -r k; do
  v1=$(awk -F= -v k="$k" '$1==k{print substr($0,index($0,"=")+1); exit}' /data/v1.conf)
  v2=$(awk -F= -v k="$k" '$1==k{print substr($0,index($0,"=")+1); exit}' /data/v2.conf)
  if [ "$v1" != "$v2" ]; then
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$k" "$v1" "$v2" >> /tmp/changed.lines
  fi
done < /tmp/common.keys

# Print human-readable summary
printf 'Configuration differences between /data/v1.conf and /data/v2.conf\n\n'
printf 'Added keys:\n'
if [ -s /tmp/added.keys ]; then
  while IFS= read -r k; do
    v=$(awk -F= -v k="$k" '$1==k{print substr($0,index($0,"=")+1); exit}' /data/v2.conf)
    printf '  - %s=%s\n' "$k" "$v"
  done < /tmp/added.keys
else
  printf '  (none)\n'
fi

printf '\nRemoved keys:\n'
if [ -s /tmp/removed.keys ]; then
  while IFS= read -r k; do
    v=$(awk -F= -v k="$k" '$1==k{print substr($0,index($0,"=")+1); exit}' /data/v1.conf)
    printf '  - %s=%s\n' "$k" "$v"
  done < /tmp/removed.keys
else
  printf '  (none)\n'
fi

printf '\nChanged values:\n'
if [ -s /tmp/changed.lines ]; then
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r k v1 v2; do
    printf '  - %s: %s -> %s\n' "$k" "$v1" "$v2"
  done < /tmp/changed.lines
else
  printf '  (none)\n'
fi

task.description: Rename a function across multiple source files
task.prompt: Rename the function 'getData' to 'fetchRecords' in all .py files under /src/. Print which files were modified and how many replacements were made in each file.

files./src/main.py:
from utils import getData

def run():
    result = getData()
    print(result)

content.text: Rename the function 'getData' to 'fetchRecords' in all .py files under /src/. Print which files were modified and how many replacements were made in each file.

input.commands:
set -euo pipefail
if [ ! -d /src ]; then
  echo "/src not found" >&2
  exit 1
fi

modified=0
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
  count=$(grep -oF 'getData' "$file" | wc -l || true)
  # trim whitespace
  count=$(echo "$count" | tr -d '[:space:]')
  if [ "${count:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
    sed -i 's/getData/fetchRecords/g' "$file"
    echo "$file: $count"
    modified=$((modified+1))
  fi
done < <(find /src -type f -name '*.py' -print0)

if [ "$modified" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No .py files required changes under /src/."
fi

cat > /scripts/healthcheck.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash

all_pass=0

# 1) /app/config.json exists and contains valid JSON
if [ -f /app/config.json ] && jq empty /app/config.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "PASS: /app/config.json exists and contains valid JSON"
else
  echo "FAIL: /app/config.json exists and contains valid JSON"
  all_pass=1
fi

# 2) /var/log directory exists
if [ -d /var/log ]; then
  echo "PASS: /var/log directory exists"
else
  echo "FAIL: /var/log directory exists"
  all_pass=1
fi

# 3) /app/data directory contains at least 2 files
file_count=$(find /app/data -maxdepth 1 -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [ "$file_count" -ge 2 ]; then
  echo "PASS: /app/data directory contains at least 2 files"

content.text: Join /data/employees.csv and /data/departments.csv on the department_id field. Produce /data/report.csv with columns: name, department_name, salary. Include a header row. Print the result.

input.commands:
set -e
# Inspect input files
echo '--- employees.csv ---'
cat /data/employees.csv
echo '--- departments.csv ---'
cat /data/departments.csv

# Build report.csv by joining on department_id
awk -F',' 'BEGIN{OFS=","}
NR==FNR { if (FNR==1) next; dept[$1]=$2; next }
FNR==1 { print "name","department_name","salary"; next }
{ print $2, dept[$3], $4 }
' /data/departments.csv /data/employees.csv > /data/report.csv

echo '--- report.csv ---'
cat /data/report.csv

task.description: Find files containing deprecated API and replace across codebase
task.prompt: Search all .js files under /src for the string 'console.log'. Replace every occurrence with 'logger.info' in-place. Print each modified filename and how many replacements were made per file. At the end, print 'Files modified: N' with the total count of files changed.

content.text: Search all .js files under /src for the string 'console.log'. Replace every occurrence with 'logger.info' in-place. Print each modified filename and how many replacements were made per file. At the end, print 'Files modified: N' with the total count of files changed.

input.commands:
set -euo pipefail
modified=0
# Find all .js files under /src
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
  count=$(grep -o 'console\.log' "$file" | wc -l || true)
  count=$(echo "$count" | tr -d '[:space:]')
  if [ "$count" -gt 0 ]; then
    sed -i 's/console\.log/logger.info/g' "$file"
    printf '%s: %s replacements\n' "$file" "$count"
    modified=$((modified + 1))
  fi
done < <(find /src -type f -name '*.js' -print0)
printf 'Files modified: %s\n' "$modified"

task.prompt: Write /scripts/start.sh that configures a service. It should read these environment variables and fall back to defaults when they're not set: DB_HOST (default 'localhost'), DB_PORT (default '5432'), DB_NAME (default 'myapp'), LOG_LEVEL (default 'info'). Print the resolved config as 'key=value' lines. If DB_HOST is explicitly set, also print 'custom_db=true'. content.text: Write /scripts/start.sh that configures a service. It should read these environment variables and fall back to defaults when they're not set: DB_HOST (default 'localhost'), DB_PORT (default '5432'), DB_NAME (default 'myapp'), LOG_LEVEL (default 'info'). Print the resolved config as 'key=value' lines. If DB_HOST is explicitly set, also print 'custom_db=true'. Run the script twice: first with no env vars set (to test defaults), then with DB_HOST=db.prod.internal to test the override.

input.commands:
set -e
mkdir -p /scripts

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

DB_HOST_RESOLVED="${DB_HOST:-localhost}"
DB_PORT_RESOLVED="${DB_PORT:-5432}"
DB_NAME_RESOLVED="${DB_NAME:-myapp}"
LOG_LEVEL_RESOLVED="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"

echo "DB_HOST=${DB_HOST_RESOLVED}"
echo "DB_PORT=${DB_PORT_RESOLVED}"
echo "DB_NAME=${DB_NAME_RESOLVED}"
echo "LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL_RESOLVED}"

if [[ -v DB_HOST ]]; then
  echo "custom_db=true"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /scripts/start.sh

echo '--- run 1: no env vars set ---'
env -u DB_HOST -u DB_PORT -u DB_NAME -u LOG_LEVEL /scripts/start.sh

echo '--- run 2: DB_HOST override ---'
env -u DB_PORT -u DB_NAME -u LOG_LEVEL DB_HOST=db.prod.internal /scripts/start.sh

task.description: Organize files by extension into categorized directories
task.prompt: Under /uploads there are files with various extensions. Write a script that: 1) For each file, determines its extension and moves it into a subdirectory named after that extension (e.g., .txt files go to /uploads/txt/, .csv files go to /uploads/csv/). Create the subdirectories as needed. 2) Print each move as 'moved: FILENAME -> EXTENSION_DIR/'. 3) After all moves, print a summary of how many files per extension as 'EXT: N files'. Handle the case where a file has no extension by putting it in /uploads/other/.

content.text: Under /uploads there are files with various extensions. Write a script that: 1) For each file, determines its extension and moves it into a subdirectory named after that extension (e.g., .txt files go to /uploads/txt/, .csv files go to /uploads/csv/). Create the subdirectories as needed. 2) Print each move as 'moved: FILENAME -> EXTENSION_DIR/'. 3) After all moves, print a summary of how many files per extension as 'EXT: N files'. Handle the case where a file has no extension by putting it in /uploads/other/.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="/uploads"
declare -A counts

# Process only regular files directly under /uploads
while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
  filename="${file##*/}"

  # Determine extension:
  # - normal files: use text after last dot
  # - no extension / trailing dot / dotfile without extra dot => "other"
  stripped="${filename#.}"   # remove one leading dot (for .hidden.txt case)
  if [[ "$stripped" == *.* ]]; then
    ext="${stripped##*.}"
    [[ -z "$ext" ]] && ext="other"
  else
    ext="other"
  fi

  dest_dir="$ROOT/$ext"
  mkdir -p -- "$dest_dir"
  mv -- "$file" "$dest_dir/"

  echo "moved: $filename -> $ext/"
  ((counts["$ext"]++))
done < <(find "$ROOT" -maxdepth 1 -type f -print0)

# Summary
for ext in $(printf '%s\n' "${!counts[@]}" | sort); do
  echo "$ext: ${counts[$ext]} files"
done
```

If you want, I can also provide a version that processes files recursively under `/uploads` (not just top-level).

task.description: Use trap for cleanup on EXIT and error handling
task.prompt: Write a bash script /scripts/deploy.sh that: 1) Creates a temp directory using mktemp -d and stores path in TMPDIR. 2) Sets a trap on EXIT that removes the temp directory and prints 'Cleanup: removed temp dir'. 3) Copies /app/config.json to the temp dir. 4) Simulates work by creating /app/deploy.log with the line 'deployed at TMPDIR_PATH' (using actual path). 5) Exits with code 0. Run the script. After it runs, verify the temp directory was cleaned up by checking it no longer exists, and verify /app/deploy.log exists.

input.commands:
mkdir -p /scripts
cat > /scripts/report.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
Usage: report.sh [-f format] [-o file] [-v] [-h]
  -f <format>  Output format: csv or json (default: csv)
  -o <file>    Output file (default: stdout)
  -v           Verbose mode
  -h           Print this usage and exit
USAGE
}

format="csv"
outfile=""
verbose=false
input_file="/data/input.csv"

while getopts ":f:o:vh" opt; do
  case "$opt" in
    f)
      format="$OPTARG"
      ;;
    o)
      outfile="$OPTARG"
      ;;
    v)
      verbose=true
      ;;
    h)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    :) 
      echo "Error: Option -$OPTARG requires an argument." >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
    \?)
      echo "Error: Invalid option -$OPTARG" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
done

if [ "$format" != "csv" ] && [ "$format" != "json" ]; then
  echo "Error: Invalid format '$format'. Use 'csv' or 'json'." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$input_file" ]; then
  echo "Error: Input file '$input_file' not found." >&2
  exit 1
fi

row_count=$(awk 'NR>1 {c++} END {print c+0}' "$input_file")

if [ "$verbose" = true ]; then
  echo "Verbose: processing $row_count rows"
fi

emit_json() {
  awk -F',' '
    NR==1 {
      for (i=1; i<=NF; i++) {
        key[i]=$i
      }
      next
    }
    {
      printf "{"
      for (i=1; i<=NF; i++) {
        val=$i
        gsub(/\\/, "\\\\", val)
        gsub(/\"/, "\\\"", val)
        printf "\"%s\":\"%s\"", key[i], val
        if (i < NF) printf ","
      }
      printf "}\n"
    }
  ' "$input_file"
}

if [ -n "$outfile" ]; then
  if [ "$format" = "csv" ]; then
    cat "$input_file" > "$outfile"
  else
    emit_json > "$outfile"
  fi
else
  if [ "$format" = "csv" ]; then
    cat "$input_file"
  else
    emit_json
  fi
fi
EOF
chmod +x /scripts/report.sh

bash /scripts/report.sh -f json -v
nl -ba /scripts/report.sh | sed -n '1,220p'

Then read /data/visitors.csv (columns: name,country_code) and for each row, look up the full country name and print 'name visited from FULL_COUNTRY_NAME'. At the end, print a count per country sorted by count descending as 'FULL_NAME: N visitors'.

  [US]='United States'
  [GB]='United Kingdom'

content.text: Under /workspace, find all .log files, then use xargs to count the total number of lines across all log files. Print 'Total log lines: N'. Also use find + xargs to grep for 'ERROR' across all log files and print 'Total errors: N'.

input.commands:
cd /workspace
# Count total lines across all .log files using find + xargs
TOTAL_LOG_LINES=$(find . -type f -name '*.log' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l 2>/dev/null | awk '{sum += $1} END {print sum+0}')

# Count total ERROR matches across all .log files using find + xargs + grep
TOTAL_ERRORS=$(find . -type f -name '*.log' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -h 'ERROR' 2>/dev/null | wc -l | awk '{print $1}')

printf "Total log lines: %s\n" "$TOTAL_LOG_LINES"
printf "Total errors: %s\n" "$TOTAL_ERRORS"

task.description: Source config file, export variables, verify environment propagation
task.prompt: Create /etc/env.conf with variable assignments: APP_ENV=production, APP_DEBUG=false, APP_SECRET=s3cret123. Source this file. Export all three variables. Then write a script /scripts/check_env.sh that reads these environment variables and prints them as 'APP_ENV=<value>' (one per line). Run the script with bash and verify the environment variables propagated correctly. Also run 'env | grep APP_' to list all APP_ prefixed env vars.

content.text: Create /etc/env.conf with variable assignments: APP_ENV=production, APP_DEBUG=false, APP_SECRET=s3cret123. Source this file. Export all three variables. Then write a script /scripts/check_env.sh that reads these environment variables and prints them as 'APP_ENV=<value>' (one per line). Run the script with bash and verify the environment variables propagated correctly. Also run 'env | grep APP_' to list all APP_ prefixed env vars.

input.commands:
set -e
mkdir -p /etc /scripts
cat > /etc/env.conf <<'EOF'
APP_ENV=production
APP_DEBUG=false
APP_SECRET=s3cret123
EOF

# Source and export variables
source /etc/env.conf
export APP_ENV APP_DEBUG APP_SECRET

# Create check script
cat > /scripts/check_env.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo "APP_ENV=${APP_ENV}"
echo "APP_DEBUG=${APP_DEBUG}"
echo "APP_SECRET=${APP_SECRET}"
EOF

chmod +x /scripts/check_env.sh

echo "Running /scripts/check_env.sh with bash:"
bash /scripts/check_env.sh

echo
echo "Listing APP_ variables from environment:"
env | grep '^APP_' | sort

echo
echo "Contents of /etc/env.conf:"
cat /etc/env.conf

content.text: Read /data/test-results.txt which contains test output in a standard format. Parse it to: 1) Count total tests, passed, and failed. 2) Extract the names of all failing tests. 3) Generate a summary report at /reports/test-summary.md with a header '# Test Summary', a line 'Total: N | Passed: N | Failed: N', and a '## Failures' section listing each failed test. Print the summary.

input.commands: ls -R /data && echo '---' && sed -n '1,200p' /data/test-results.txt

content.content:
/data:
test-results.txt
---
PASS test_login_valid
PASS test_login_invalid_password
FAIL test_login_expired_token
PASS test_signup_new_user
FAIL test_signup_duplicate_email
PASS test_logout
PASS test_password_reset
FAIL test_session_timeout
PASS test_profile_update
PASS test_profile_delete
PASS test_api_rate_limit
PASS test_api_auth_header

input.commands:
mkdir -p /reports
input=/data/test-results.txt
summary=/reports/test-summary.md

# Compute counts
read total passed failed <<EOF
$(awk '/^(PASS|FAIL)[[:space:]]+/ {total++; if($1=="PASS") p++; else if($1=="FAIL") f++} END {print total+0, p+0, f+0}' "$input")
EOF

# Build summary file
{
  echo "# Test Summary"
  echo
  echo "Total: $total | Passed: $passed | Failed: $failed"
  echo
  echo "## Failures"
  awk '/^FAIL[[:space:]]+/ {print "- "$2}' "$input"
} > "$summary"

# Print the summary
cat "$summary"

## Failures
- test_login_expired_token
- test_signup_duplicate_email
- test_session_timeout

task.description: Generate .env file from template with defaults
task.prompt: Read /config/template.env which has lines like 'KEY=${VALUE:-default}'. For each line, check if the key exists in /config/overrides.txt (format: KEY=value). If it does, use the override value; otherwise use the default from the template. Write the final KEY=value pairs to /app/.env and print the result.

content.text: Read /config/template.env which has lines like 'KEY=${VALUE:-default}'. For each line, check if the key exists in /config/overrides.txt (format: KEY=value). If it does, use the override value; otherwise use the default from the template. Write the final KEY=value pairs to /app/.env and print the result.

input.commands:
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p /app
: > /app/.env

while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
  line="${line%$'\r'}"
  # Skip empty and comment lines
  [[ -z "${line//[[:space:]]/}" ]] && continue
  [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue

  key_part="${line%%=*}"
  key="$(printf '%s' "$key_part" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//; s/[[:space:]]*$//')"
  rhs="${line#*=}"
  rhs="$(printf '%s' "$rhs" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//; s/[[:space:]]*$//')"

  default="$rhs"
  if [[ "$rhs" =~ ^\$\{[^:}]+:-([^}]*)\}$ ]]; then
    default="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
  fi

  override="$(awk -F'=' -v k="$key" '$1==k { print substr($0, index($0, "=")+1); exit }' /config/overrides.txt)"

  if [[ -n "$override" || $(grep -c -m1 "^${key}=" /config/overrides.txt || true) -gt 0 ]]; then
    value="$override"
  else
    value="$default"
  fi

  printf '%s=%s\n' "$key" "$value" >> /app/.env
done < /config/template.env

cat /app/.env

task.description: Multi-stage build pipeline with dependency checking
task.prompt: Simulate a build pipeline: 1) Check that /src/main.c and /src/utils.c exist, 2) 'Compile' each .c file by copying it to /build/ with a .o extension and prepending '// compiled', 3) 'Link' by concatenating all .o files into /build/program with a '// linked' header, 4) 'Package' by creating a tar.gz of /build/ at /dist/release.tar.gz. Print status for each stage. If any stage fails, stop and report the error.

content.text: Simulate a build pipeline: 1) Check that /src/main.c and /src/utils.c exist, 2) 'Compile' each .c file by copying it to /build/ with a .o extension and prepending '// compiled', 3) 'Link' by concatenating all .o files into /build/program with a '// linked' header, 4) 'Package' by creating a tar.gz of /build/ at /dist/release.tar.gz. Print status for each stage. If any stage fails, stop and report the error.

input.commands:
set -u

fail() {
  echo "ERROR: $1"
  exit 1
}

echo "[1/4] Checking source files..."
[ -f /src/main.c ] || fail "/src/main.c is missing"
[ -f /src/utils.c ] || fail "/src/utils.c is missing"
echo "OK: required source files found"

echo "[2/4] Compiling .c files to /build/*.o ..."
mkdir -p /build || fail "could not create /build"

found_c=0
for cfile in /src/*.c; do
  [ -e "$cfile" ] || continue
  found_c=1
  base=$(basename "$cfile" .c)
  ofile="/build/${base}.o"
  {
    echo "// compiled"
    cat "$cfile"
  } > "$ofile" || fail "failed to compile $cfile"
  echo "Compiled: $cfile -> $ofile"
done

[ "$found_c" -eq 1 ] || fail "no .c files found in /src"
echo "OK: compilation stage complete"

echo "[3/4] Linking object files into /build/program ..."
ls /build/*.o >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "no object files to link"
{
  echo "// linked"
  for ofile in /build/*.o; do
    cat "$ofile" || fail "failed reading $ofile during link"
  done
} > /build/program || fail "failed to write /build/program"
echo "OK: linked program created at /build/program"

echo "[4/4] Packaging build into /dist/release.tar.gz ..."
mkdir -p /dist || fail "could not create /dist"
tar -czf /dist/release.tar.gz -C / build || fail "failed to create tar.gz package"
echo "OK: package created at /dist/release.tar.gz"

echo "Pipeline completed successfully."

task.description: Generate a Makefile-like build script from dependency spec
task.prompt: Read /project/deps.txt which lists build targets and their dependencies (format: 'target: dep1 dep2'). Generate /project/build.sh that builds targets in correct dependency order (dependencies before dependents). Then run the build script, which should create each target as a file in /project/out/ containing 'built: <target>'. Print the build order.

files./project/deps.txt:
app: lib utils
lib: core
utils: core
core:

content.text: Read /project/deps.txt which lists build targets and their dependencies (format: 'target: dep1 dep2'). Generate /project/build.sh that builds targets in correct dependency order (dependencies before dependents). Then run the build script, which should create each target as a file in /project/out/ containing 'built: <target>'. Print the build order.

input.commands: ls -R /project && echo '---' && cat /project/deps.txt

content.content:
/project:
deps.txt
src

/project/src:
app
core
lib
utils
---
app: lib utils
lib: core
utils: core
core:

input.commands:
cat > /project/build.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

DEPS_FILE="/project/deps.txt"
OUT_DIR="/project/out"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

declare -A deps_map
declare -A built
declare -A visiting
declare -a targets
declare -a order

trim() {
  local s="$1"
  # trim leading whitespace
  s="${s#"${s%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
  # trim trailing whitespace
  s="${s%"${s##*[![:space:]]}"}"
  printf '%s' "$s"
}

while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
  [[ -z "${line//[[:space:]]/}" ]] && continue

  target_part="${line%%:*}"
  deps_part="${line#*:}"

  target="$(trim "$target_part")"
  deps="$(trim "$deps_part")"

  targets+=("$target")
  deps_map["$target"]="$deps"
done < "$DEPS_FILE"

build_target() {
  local t="$1"

  [[ -n "${built[$t]:-}" ]] && return 0

  if [[ -n "${visiting[$t]:-}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: cyclic dependency detected at '$t'" >&2
    exit 1
  fi

  visiting["$t"]=1

  local d
  for d in ${deps_map[$t]:-}; do
    build_target "$d"
  done

  printf 'built: %s\n' "$t" > "$OUT_DIR/$t"
  built["$t"]=1
  order+=("$t")

  unset 'visiting[$t]'
}

for t in "${targets[@]}"; do
  build_target "$t"
done

echo "Build order: ${order[*]}"
EOF

chmod +x /project/build.sh
/project/build.sh

echo '--- out files ---'
ls -1 /project/out

echo '--- contents ---'
for f in /project/out/*; do
  printf '%s: ' "$(basename "$f")"
  cat "$f"
done

content.content: Exit code: 1

input.commands: bash -x /project/build.sh

+ mkdir -p /project/out
+ declare -A deps_map

+ declare -a order

Exit code: 1
